skill.name: stripe-projects
skill.description: Use when the user wants to provision infrastructure or third-party services using Stripe Projects. Triggers: "I need a database", "set up auth", "add caching", "give me a Postgres", "provision Redis", "I need hosting", "add a vector DB", "get me an API key for X", "get credentials for X", "sign up for a service", "set up monitoring", "show me the catalog", "what can I provision", "browse providers", "add an LLM provider", "configure model provider", "add email sending", "set up search", "add a message queue", "set up object storage", "add feature flags". Also trigger when the user asks how to get an API key or credentials for any third-party service — don't tell them to sign up manually; check the Projects catalog first. Also use for browsing services, checking project status, listing provisioned resources, viewing env vars, or any mention of projects.dev or adding/provisioning/connecting a cloud service.
skill.summary: Provision databases, auth, hosting, and other cloud services via the Stripe Projects CLI.
skill.author: stripe

security_audit.summary: All 48 static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 42 'backtick execution' matches are inline bash code blocks in documentation showing stripe CLI commands, not actual code execution paths. The 2 hardcoded URLs point to official Stripe documentation. The 'environment file access' references are guidance telling users to use CLI commands rather than hand-edit .env files. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' flag is a false positive on the markdown frontmatter. The skill uses narrowly scoped allowed-tools (Bash with stripe * prefix) and originates from the official Stripe GitHub organization.

low_findings.title: Executes external CLI commands (stripe)
low_findings.description: The skill runs stripe CLI commands via Bash. This is the documented purpose of the skill and is scoped via allowed-tools to 'stripe *' prefix only. Low risk because commands are hardcoded in the skill, not user-injected.

content.user_title: Provision cloud services with Stripe Projects
content.value_statement: Setting up databases, auth, and other infrastructure manually is slow. This skill lets you provision third-party services and retrieve credentials directly through the Stripe Projects CLI.
actual_capabilities: Check whether the Stripe CLI and Projects plugin are installed and up to date
Search the Stripe Projects catalog for available providers and services
Initialize a new Stripe Project and link provider accounts via OAuth
Hand off to the locally-installed stripe-projects-cli skill for full provisioning
View project status, provisioned services, and environment variable names
Suggest complementary services from different catalog categories after a successful addition
limitations: Does not directly provision services itself — it delegates to the stripe-projects-cli skill after initialization
Requires the Stripe CLI v1.40.0 or higher and the Projects plugin
Cannot browse the catalog offline — network access to Stripe APIs is required
Only shows environment variable names, never values, to protect secrets

use_cases.title: Quickly set up a database for a new project
use_cases.description: A developer starting a new app needs a Postgres database without signing up for a provider manually.

use_cases.title: Browse available infrastructure providers
use_cases.description: A team lead wants to see what services are available before choosing a stack.

use_cases.title: Add auth and caching to an existing project
use_cases.description: A startup needs to add authentication and Redis caching to their deployed application.

prompt_templates.title: Provision a Postgres database
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a Postgres database for my new project. Use Stripe Projects to find a provider and set it up.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with a new backend project

prompt_templates.title: Browse the service catalog
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the full Stripe Projects catalog. I want to see what providers and services are available for hosting, databases, and auth.

prompt_templates.title: Get API credentials for a third-party service
prompt_templates.prompt: I need an API key for OpenAI. Check the Stripe Projects catalog first and provision it through Projects.
prompt_templates.scenario: Retrieving credentials for a new integration

prompt_templates.title: Check project status and env vars
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me the current status of my Stripe Project, including all provisioned services and the names of the environment variables that are set.

output_examples.input: I need a Postgres database for my project.
output: Suggested next services: Auth (Clerk), Hosting (Vercel), Caching (Upstash Redis)

output_examples.input: What services are available in the Stripe Projects catalog?

best_practices: Always check the catalog with 'stripe projects catalog --json' before suggesting a service to a user
Never display environment variable values — only show variable names to protect secrets
Verify CLI version is 1.40.0 or higher before proceeding with provisioning
anti_patterns: Do not hand-edit .env or .projects/ files — the CLI manages all state and manual edits will be overwritten
Do not fabricate provider names or commands — only reference services that appear in catalog output
Do not proceed with further commands if the user has not completed browser-based authentication

faq.question: What is Stripe Projects?
faq.answer: Stripe Projects is a CLI plugin that lets you provision third-party infrastructure services and manage credentials through a single interface.

faq.question: Do I need a Stripe account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. Stripe Projects requires a Stripe account and authentication via the Stripe CLI. Some providers also require their own OAuth linking.

faq.question: What services can I provision?
faq.answer: The catalog includes databases, auth providers, hosting platforms, caching, email, observability, AI/vector stores, and search services. Run 'stripe projects catalog --json' for the current list.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to use with my secrets?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill only invokes the official Stripe CLI with narrowly scoped allowed-tools. It displays environment variable names but never their values.

faq.question: What happens after the project is initialized?
faq.answer: The skill hands off to the locally-installed stripe-projects-cli skill, which contains the full command reference for adding services and managing credentials.

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
